title: Agentic Assurance Profile residual register
description: Schema for a residual register (default location `assurance/RESIDUALS.yaml`). Each entry is a known limitation, unverified assumption, accepted inconsistency, unsupported condition, or remaining doubt (PROFILE.md sections 2 and 12). `Critical residual` means `impact: critical`; the separate uncertainty axis does not trigger that gate. Every ACCEPTED disposition records its human authority, date, and rationale; carrying a critical residual requires explicit human-owner acceptance, while RESOLVED records resolution grounds. A residual must not be closed solely because no recent incident was observed.

id.description: Stable residual ID, independent of GitHub Issue numbers.

summary.description: Safe public summary of the remaining uncertainty.

private_detail_location.description: Bounded non-actionable identifier for where restricted detail lives, or null when there is none. Never a private URL, repository path, account, token, or sensitive identifier. Use only ASCII letters, digits, dot, underscore, and hyphen.

affected_claims.description: IDs of claims this residual affects.

affected_invariants.description: IDs of invariants this residual affects.

impact.description: Impact if the residual materializes. `critical` is the profile's definition of a critical residual and triggers its authority and conformance rules.

uncertainty.description: Uncertainty about the residual's assessment. This is independent of impact; `critical` uncertainty does not by itself make a non-critical impact a critical residual.

status.description: Residual status: OPEN (unaccepted and unresolved), ACCEPTED (explicitly accepted by the acceptance authority), RESOLVED (no longer applies, with evidence).

owner.description: Named human owner of the residual.

accepted_by.description: The human acceptance authority, or null when not accepted.

accepted_at.description: Civil acceptance date (YYYY-MM-DD), or null when not accepted. A recorded acceptance cannot be future-dated; because the value carries no offset, the validator allows the date currently possible in UTC+14.

acceptance_rationale.description: The human acceptor's recorded reason for an ACCEPTED disposition; required when status is ACCEPTED.

resolution_note.description: How a RESOLVED residual was closed (remediation reference); required when status is RESOLVED.

review_after.description: Date (YYYY-MM-DD) after which the residual must be reviewed.

then.$comment: Every ACCEPTED disposition is a recorded human decision and requires an authority, non-future acceptance date, and rationale. PROFILE.md reserves acceptance of critical residuals to the human owner. A RESOLVED residual instead carries resolution grounds.
A RESOLVED residual requires non-blank resolution grounds.
